reviews.review_title: Great playability and art style, but...
reviews.review_description:
First of I have to say that I really like the art style. There is something alien about it. The game also plays really well and runs smooth on my Quest 2.

Unfortunately the difficulty is just too much for me. I am basicly stuck on the third arena where I am constantly killed by the flying worms. I just don't understand how I am sopposed to make the portal appear. And where is it located? I feel that this game would have been helped by a difficulty setting.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! The portal appears on its own after a minute or so, but that third arena is particularly tough. You can grab the sacrifice ability from the second arena which converts orbs into health. It helps surviving through the third arena a lot. Also when all else fails, you can just avoid enemies and wait for the portal!

reviews.review_description: The menu comes up, but I can’t interact with it. Not with my controllers, not with hand tracking. Is there some third way I should be trying to press buttons on this menu? If so, it should definitely be explained. I have pro controllers, is that the problem? I mean, it shouldn’t be…

developer_response.body: Unfortunately our game doesn't currently support the pro controllers. This is something we can looking into adding support for in our next update though!

reviews.review_description:
Vex Mage is a fast-paced roguelite that absolutely nails the balance between accessibility and depth. The sheer number of gameplay mechanics, weapon options, and possible combinations is mind-blowing, offering endless ways to experiment and strategize.

Each run feels unique thanks to the solid variety of levels, enemies, and an intriguing storyline that keeps you curious about what’s next. It’s easy to pick up but gradually ramps up in challenge and complexity, rewarding skill and persistence.

Performance is impressively stable even with so much happening on screen, and the overall execution feels polished and purposeful. This is the kind of VR game you’ll keep coming back to — discovering new strategies, mastering your craft, and having fun every single time.

Of all the roguelikes/roguelites I've played on VR so far (and there have been quite a few lol), this is by far the coolest looking. Like most games of this genre, it's incredibly addictive too. I keep dying after entering the portal after the first area, but hopefully I get the hang of it enough to get further. The game looks beautiful, the magic is pretty good (granted, I only understand one of the runes because I didn't do the training mode yet lol), and it's really fast paced. I'm actually surprised at how fast you can move with all that's going on in the game, which is also a plus. I recommend this one. Worth the money

reviews.review_title: Solid game, probably the next vr staple
reviews.review_description:
As the title suggests vex mage is a really solid game, no bs or insanely complex mechanics. Just a simple and solid rougelike with neat spell mechanics.

That being said, I have two points that are important to consider;

1: its a rougelike and a hard, fast paced one at that. Meaning you'll die, a lot and need the tutorials. It also means the gameplay can get a tad bit repetative espessially considering there is only three spells (not dissing them though because all three are fun and the combinations are cool)

2: motion sickness, if you are prone to it be careful and possibly pass on this because of the fast paced nature of the game.

Ultimately vex mage looks and feels like a great addition to any vr collection and belongs in the same tier as blade and sorcery or superhot as classic single player games.

Wow after seeing all these awesome reviews. I thought o would try this game. Probably the Devs making the reviews, as this was the most boring wave shooter crap. If you want a basic game fine. That’s what you get here. All this wave based trash is so old. This is a game from a triple A company, yet I’ve seen indie games way more fun. Sorry devs you may be offended by my opinions, but I don’t give a crap. It’s called conservative criticism. Take what you can, and god sakes make it better.

developer_response.body: Spicy! For real though, appreciate the feedback anyway and sorry the game didn't live up to your expectations. For what it's worth, our goal was to make a game with depth in mechanics and skill mastery, where the content is almost entirely accessible immediately without any artificial gates. Also, we (Nervous Systems) are a small team of three and definitely aren't posting reviews. We're happy to take the criticism though and will be working on adding fixes and features based on feedback we continue to get!

reviews.review_description: This game is very cool and is a fun and unique experience.  The floating/flying and gliding mixed with the elemental conjuring while sawing thru the different types of enemies is just insanely fun.  One of those easy to jump into but difficult to master.  There is definitely a technique involved that takes some learning and time to get used to.  This is a great game and congratulations to the developers on this.  Great job and excited for the future of this one!
its Incredibly fun. When you get you focus bar up, it oh-so Satisfying  to weave around and through you enemies attacks. I really recommend you get this game if you enjoy FAST shooters.
The game is fun so far but, I paid for the game and it says I only have the demo.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! This is a bug where a tooltip for online mode says it's unavailable in the demo when online mode has not yet been unlocked. Online mode will unlock once you've completed onboarding by unlocking all the spell runes. We'll push a fix for this soon!

reviews.review_description: This game does not have much content (that I know of) yet but it has probably the most creative fighting mechanic I have seen in every game. Also I love fast games

As cool as this game is & all, can I at least have an option where I can have a player body? or at least have a visible player body for my character so I'm not just a head and hands? Makes it more immersive imo.
I love the gameplay loop it's simple but fun kill monsters escape through the portal die retry make it a little farther repeat and I think this game is probably one of my favorites
this game is the perfect road like and first person shooter and best part you can play with friends
It was fun up until I was stuck on the emerge board. Is this because I have a quest 2 or is there a hidden task that needs to be completed?

developer_response.body: This sounds like a bug! You should just need to collect enough orbs to unlock all of your runes. Send an email to us at support@nervous-systems.com or jump in our Discord so we can help narrow down the issue.
